section.heading: Annual report on purchase of drop-in fuel

subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.chapeau: Not less frequently than annually, the Secretary of Defense shall submit to Congress a report that, for the year covered by the report—

paragraph.content: identifies each instance in which the Secretary purchased drop-in fuel that was not cost-competitive with traditional fuel; and
for each instance identified under paragraph (1), states whether the purchase was based on a military requirement or not.

The term “drop-in fuel” means a neat or blended liquid hydrocarbon fuel designed as a direct replacement for a traditional fuel with comparable performance characteristics and compatible with existing infrastructure and equipment.
The term “traditional fuel” means a liquid hydrocarbon fuel derived or refined from petroleum.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 118–159, div. A, title III, § 315(b), Dec. 23, 2024, 138 Stat. 1852.)
